content: POST routes for parameterized paths like /api/strategy/:id/approve go before the 'if (request.method \!== GET)' guard in routes.ts. GET counterparts go in the GET section after audit routes. Use block scope {} + matchRoute() for parameterized POST routes.
routes.test.ts: sessions.bead_id is NOT NULL constrained — cannot pass beadId: null when seeding coordinator sessions in tests. Use a dummy string like 'coord-task' instead.

WebSocket push events use broadcastEvent(event) on WebSocketManager; routes.ts accepts optional wsManager param (minimal interface, not full WebSocketManager) to avoid circular imports; index.ts threads the wsManager through the dynamic import call site.

runLegio helper pattern in routes.ts: extract spawn boilerplate into async function that returns {ok: true, data: unknown} | {ok: false, error: string}. Use proc.stdout?.on('data') with optional chaining since stdio is 'pipe' not 'inherit'. Falls back to stdout.trim() if JSON.parse fails on stdout.

parseJsonBody helper in routes.ts: returns Record<string,unknown> | Response. Callers check 'if (parsed instanceof Response) return parsed' to short-circuit. Keeps body validation DRY across POST endpoints.

New POST API routes order in routes.ts: POST /api/coordinator/start, POST /api/coordinator/stop, POST /api/agents/spawn (201 Created), POST /api/merge, POST /api/nudge — all added before the GET-only guard. Each calls runLegio with --json flag and wsManager?.broadcastEvent() on success.

routes.test.ts for legio CLI endpoints: tests for POST /api/coordinator/start|stop, /api/agents/spawn, /api/merge, /api/nudge accept [200,500] or [201,500] status codes since legio CLI may fail in test env. Validation 400 tests are always deterministic. wsManager broadcast tests are conditional on response status (only verify event if success).

runLegio(args, projectRoot) is a shared helper in routes.ts for spawning legio CLI as subprocess and parsing JSON output. All POST endpoints that wrap legio CLI commands should use this helper instead of inline spawn boilerplate.

POST API endpoints in routes.ts that wrap legio CLI commands follow pattern: parseJsonBody → validate required fields → build args array → runLegio(args, projectRoot) → wsManager?.broadcastEvent on success → jsonResponse. All POST routes go before the GET-only guard.

MailAudience type (human|agent|both) on MailMessage: SQLite migration uses ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for tables with CHECK+payload+protocol but lacking audience; full recreation for older schemas. MailClient.send() defaults audience to 'agent' for protocol types and 'both' for semantic types based on PROTOCOL_TYPES Set.

mail --audience flag: VALID_AUDIENCES = ['human','agent','both'] as const. AGENT_AUDIENCE_TYPES set drives auto-derive (protocol types -> 'agent', semantic types -> 'both'). handleSend uses SendWithAudience cast to pass audience to client.send() until schema-lead adds field to MailClient interface. Filtering in handleCheck and handleList uses (m as unknown as Record<string, unknown>).audience to avoid TS2352 overlap error.

handleCheck --audience inject path: must use openStore() directly (not client.checkInject) to mark only audience-filtered messages as read. Requires local formatMessagesForInjection() helper in mail.ts since formatForInjection() in client.ts is not exported.

GET /api/mail/conversations fetches all messages then filters in-memory by audience before grouping. Functionally correct for current scale; consider pushing audience filter to store.getAll() for large mail stores.
GET /api/coordinator/status returns { running: boolean, tmuxSession?: string } — not state:'running'|'stopped'. Consumers (CLI, frontend CoordinatorBar) check data?.running === true.

MetricsStore time filtering and grouping: getSessionsFiltered(opts) uses parameterized SQL with NULL-safe comparisons ($since IS NULL OR started_at >= $since); getSessionsByModel/getSessionsByDate use GROUP BY with COALESCE(model_used,'unknown') and DATE(started_at) respectively. Routes /api/metrics/by-model and /api/metrics/by-date follow the same open/try/finally/close pattern as existing metrics routes. since/until route params use url.searchParams.get() ?? undefined, then pass to store as null when absent.

ChatStore (chat-store.ts) follows audit-store.ts pattern exactly: better-sqlite3, WAL mode, busy_timeout=5000, foreign_keys=ON for cascade deletes, crypto.randomUUID() for IDs, snake_case SQLite columns mapped to camelCase TypeScript interfaces.

Chat API routes in routes.ts: GET /api/chat/config, GET /api/chat/sessions, POST /api/chat/sessions, DELETE /api/chat/sessions/:id, GET /api/chat/sessions/:id/messages, POST /api/chat/sessions/:id/messages. Claude API call is an inline helper callClaudeApi() in routes.ts — not a separate module. API key from process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. 502 returned when key missing or API fails.

Raw Claude chat backend: chat-store.ts follows audit-store.ts pattern (better-sqlite3, WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, per-request lifecycle). Routes use callClaudeApi() inline helper with native fetch against https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages, API key from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var. GET /api/chat/config exposes { available, defaultModel } for frontend to check configuration.

HeadlessCoordinator uses the 'script' command as a PTY wrapper (macOS: script -q /dev/null -c <cmd>, Linux: script -qfc <cmd> /dev/null) to run Claude Code without tmux; output is buffered in a ring buffer and broadcast via WebSocket 'coordinator_output' events.

HeadlessCoordinatorHandle DI interface pattern: on() method uses exact literal event type ('exit') and typed listener (code: number). Fake test implementations must match this signature exactly, wrapping the emitter.on call with (...args: unknown[]) => { listener(args[0] as number); } to satisfy TypeScript strictFunctionTypes.

HeadlessCoordinator session marker: sessions started with --headless flag use tmuxSession='headless' string (not a real tmux session name). stopCoordinator and statusCoordinator detect headless sessions by checking session.tmuxSession === 'headless' and use PID-based lifecycle (.legio/headless-coordinator.pid) instead of tmux commands.

Fake HeadlessCoordinatorHandle test helper: use setTimeout(fn, 50) not process.nextTick() to emit 'exit'. startCoordinator does async work (writeFile, mail send) between start() and registering the exit listener, so the exit must fire after those awaits complete.

MetricsStore time filtering with getSessionsFiltered/getSessionsByModel/getSessionsByDate: WHERE clause uses OR completed_at >= $since OR completed_at IS NULL to capture sessions active during window (not just started during it). The $since IS NULL guard and $until filter (started_at <= $until) remain unchanged.

Dynamic import (await import()) inside HTTP request handlers caches the ESM module from first load. If routes.ts is updated after first load, the stale cached module is used for all subsequent requests. Static top-level import resolves once at startup from current disk, preventing stale-cache 405 errors on newly-added POST routes.

MetricsStore run_id migration pattern: add run_id TEXT to TOKEN_COLUMNS array (not a separate migration) so it gets added by migrateTokenColumns() automatically. Insert stmt, SessionRow interface, rowToMetrics, and recordSession all updated together. New getSessionsByRun(runId) method uses prepared statement with $run_id param. costs --run now calls metricsStore.getSessionsByRun() directly — no SessionStore cross-reference needed.

TaskDetailView data loading: fetch /api/issues/:id + /api/agents + /api/mail in parallel on mount; poll every 5s via setInterval in useEffect cleanup ref pattern. useCallback on fetchAll with taskId dep prevents stale closure.
getRecentMessages uses ORDER BY created_at DESC, rowid DESC LIMIT $limit then .reverse() — the rowid secondary sort ensures stable ordering within the same SQLite datetime second (1-second precision)

COORDINATOR_SESSION_ID='coordinator' is a well-known fixed ID exported from chat-store.ts; getOrCreateCoordinatorSession() inserts with fixed id instead of UUID so the same session survives across store open/close cycles

ANSI escape code stripping in routes.ts: use biome-ignore lint/suspicious/noControlCharactersInRegex comment above the regex literal when matching ESC sequences — biome flags both \x1b and \u001b as control characters in regex

captureCoordinatorResponse pattern: fire-and-forget background task (void fn()) that polls captureTmuxPane with POLL_INTERVAL=1500ms, detects stabilization after STABLE_THRESHOLD=3000ms no-change, extracts delta via diffCaptureServer(), strips ANSI, persists assistant message to chat.db, broadcasts coordinator_chat_response WS event. Must open its own ChatStore (request handler's store is closed before bg task completes).

POST /api/agents/:name/chat and GET /api/agents/:name/chat/history: store human-to-agent chat messages in mail.db with from='human', to=agentName, audience='human', type='status', subject='chat'. History endpoint uses getAll({from:'human', to:agentName, audience:'human'}) then .slice(0,limit).reverse() for chronological order.

POST /api/coordinator/chat migrated from chat.db to mail.db: user messages stored as MailMessage (from:'human', to:'coordinator', audience:'human'). GET /api/coordinator/chat/history similarly reads from mail.db. Both old coordinator endpoints kept as working aliases.

Chat store timestamps are ISO 8601 TEXT strings auto-set via datetime('now') in SQLite DEFAULT clauses. Frontend mapHistoryMessage() passes through createdAt field as-is. All DB updates (updated_at on message insert) use manual UPDATE statements.

GET /api/chat/unified/history endpoint: queries mail.db for audience=human + audience=both separately then deduplicates with Set in-memory, rather than SQL OR — intentional to reuse existing mail store getAll({audience}) filter API.

GET /api/chat/unified/history endpoint implementation: queries mail.db twice (getAll({audience:'human'}) + getAll({audience:'both'})), deduplicates with Set<string> on id, sorts by createdAt.localeCompare(), returns slice(-limit). Default limit=200. Returns raw MailMessage objects (from/to/body/createdAt/id fields).

Ideas CRUD API pattern in routes.ts: Idea/IdeasFile interfaces defined locally (before helpers, after imports) with TODO comment to move to types.ts. GET returns empty array if ideas.json missing. POST creates with randomUUID().slice(0,8) id. PUT/DELETE use matchRoute + method check inside same block. dispatch uses createMailStore to insert message; backlog uses createBeadsClient.create(). All routes follow existing pattern: read file, mutate, writeFile, jsonResponse.

GET /api/chat/unified/history bidirectional fix: query by from='human' and to='human' separately, deduplicate with Set<string>, then filter by audience==='human'||'both'. Old pattern queried by audience only and missed messages where from/to=human but audience not tagged.

POST /api/chat/transcript-sync route pattern: uses openSessionStore(legioDir) (not openSessionStore(sessionsDbPath)) and destructures {store: sessionStore}; checks fileExists(join(legioDir,'sessions.db')) for early 404; reads .transcript-path cache + .chat-transcript-offset; dynamic import parseTranscriptTexts; inserts assistant messages to mail.db with audience='human'; writes nextLine offset.

POST /api/gateway/chat mirrors coordinator pattern: createMailStore, insert {from:'human', to:'gateway', subject:'chat', audience:'human'}, close, then sendKeys. Returns savedMessage (201). GET /api/gateway/chat/history mirrors coordinator/chat/history: queries humanToGateway + gatewayToHuman, deduplicates, filters audience=human|both, sorts chronologically, slices -limit.

POST /api/gateway/chat and POST /api/chat/transcript-sync both broadcast wsManager?.broadcastEvent({ type: 'mail_new', data: savedMsg }) after each mail.db insert, enabling instant WS push to clients. Pattern mirrors POST /api/mail.

POST /api/issues/:id/dispatch endpoint pattern: use createBeadsClient(projectRoot).show(id) to fetch issue, then createMailStore(join(legioDir,'mail.db')).insert({id:messageId, from:'human', to:'coordinator', subject:'dispatch: <title>', body, type:'dispatch', priority:'normal', threadId:null, audience:'agent'}), then store.close(). Return jsonResponse({success:true, message:'Dispatched'}). Place this route before the GET-only guard.

POST /api/issues/:id/dispatch endpoint pattern: fetch issue via beads client.show(), insert dispatch mail to coordinator with from='human', subject='dispatch: <title>', type='dispatch', audience='agent'. Placed BEFORE the GET-only guard in routes.ts. Returns {success:true} or 404/500.

POST /api/chat/transcript-sync dedup pattern: mailStore is created before the offset block so it is available for both offset recovery and message dedup. Recovery: if offset file missing but messages exist (getAll({from,to:'human'}).length>0), parse transcript to get endLine, write offset, return {synced:0,skipped:true,reason:'offset_recovered'}. Dedup: before insert loop, build recentBodies Set from getAll filtered to last 24h; skip messages whose body is already in the set.

Module-level cache pattern for /api/issues in routes.ts: declare let issuesCacheData: unknown = null, let issuesCacheAt = 0, const ISSUES_CACHE_TTL = 3000 at module scope after IdeasFile interface. isDefaultRequest = !statusParam && all && !limit gates cache usage. Cache populated only on default requests, bypassed for filtered queries.

POST /api/issues/:id/close endpoint pattern: place BEFORE the GET-only guard (same as dispatch). Use createBeadsClient(projectRoot).close(id, reason). Read optional { reason } from request body via request.json().catch(()=>({})), default to 'Closed from dashboard'. Return jsonResponse({ success: true, id }). Error handling mirrors dispatch: check 'not found' in error message for 404.
/api/agents route uses run-aware filtering: reads current-run.txt, calls getByRunIncludeOrphans(runId) when present, falls back to getActive(). Matches gatherStatus() pattern in status.ts.
routes.test.ts uses vi.stubGlobal; must run with npx vitest run, not bun test (bun gives vi.stubGlobal undefined).
